blurb: The UK AI Security Institute (formerly AI Safety Institute) — government body running frontier-model evaluations (cyber/chem/bio/agent) and publishing safety-framework and safety-case research. High-signal, named-author technical-policy output.

publications.title: Research Agenda
publications.summary: AISI sets out its research priorities and approach to technical solutions for the most pressing AI risks as capabilities advance — the closest thing to a UK government statement of frontier-AI-risk posture.

publications.title: Our evaluation of OpenAI's GPT-5.5 cyber capabilities
publications.summary: AISI cyber evaluation finding GPT-5.5 among the strongest models tested, and the second to solve a multi-step cyber-attack simulation end-to-end — a concrete capability-evaluation data point feeding deployment policy.

publications.title: Conference on frontier AI safety frameworks
publications.summary: AISI convenes AI companies and researchers to accelerate the design and implementation of frontier AI safety frameworks — government-led coordination on RSP-style commitments.

publications.title: Safety cases at AISI
publications.summary: AISI sketches structured 'safety case' arguments for more advanced models than exist today, focused on loss-of-control and autonomy risk — a methodological bet on how frontier safety should be evidenced.

notes: [publications backfill 2026-06-26] Harvested from the org's own publications page. Some items lack a visible date on the index — null rather than fabricated; refine on a later pass.
